summary: DeVault declares the web effectively dead, arguing that browsers have expanded far beyond reasonable scope into obscene territory. He points to Google's self-serving proposals like AMP, Mozilla's mismanagement and layoffs, Microsoft's surrender, and Safari's irrelevance as evidence that no good steward of the open web remains. He calls for an immediate freeze on new browser APIs and demands that vendors start cutting features rather than adding them, refocusing on performance, security, and reliability within existing scope.
key_points: Browser scope has grown to an unmanageable degree, with features like WebUSB, WebBluetooth, WebXR, and Encrypted Media Extensions representing unnecessary bloat
Every major browser vendor has failed: Google serves its own interests, Mozilla fired its technical talent to chase fads, Microsoft gave up, and Safari remains closed and single-platform
New browser APIs should be frozen indefinitely while vendors focus on performance, efficiency, reliability, and security of existing features
Corporate management bears responsibility for browser decay, collecting large paychecks while laying off the engineers who do meaningful work
Engineers working on harmful projects like AMP share personal responsibility for the damage those projects cause
money_quotes: The web and web browsers have become Lovecraftian horrors of an unprecedented scale.
They've long since left "scope creep" territory and entered "oh my god please just stop" territory.
Google is all that's left, and they're not a good steward of the open web. The browsers are drowning under their own scope. The web is dead.
I call for an immediate and indefinite suspension of the addition of new developer-facing APIs to web browsers.
Browser vendors need to start thinking about reducing scope and cutting features.
At some point you need to stop adding scope and start focusing on performance, efficiency, reliability, and security at the scope you already have.
If you're a Google engineer who is still working on AMP, you are a disgrace to your field.
key_insight: Web browsers have become so bloated with unnecessary APIs and corporate agendas that the only path forward is to freeze new features entirely and aggressively reduce scope.
